it's very sad to see how hacks implemented as a temporary very local solution to work ldiskfs specific weakness around infect other clean-before components.

Patch Set 12:

> it's very sad to see how hacks implemented as a temporary very local solution to work ldiskfs specific weakness around infect other clean-before components.

The new logic for the osd-zfs only affects the system migrated from old  ldiskfs based OST with combined EA. And the zfs-osd only splits the PFID EA from combined EA, not generate more combined EA.

If we want to support the system migration from ldiskfs to ZFS, and if you do not want osd-zfs to touch related combined EA, then another option is to split the combined EA on the ldiskfs before the ZFS migration. But that requires the user to migrate the OST from ldiskfs small-sized inode device to ldiskfs large-sized indie device. From the user view, it is inconvenient than the direct migration from ldiskfs to ZFS. That is why I patch the osd-zfs.

Patch Set 12:

the right option is to leave ldiskfs specifics in osd-ldiskfs and zfs specifics in osd-zfs.

Patch Set 12:

> the right option is to leave ldiskfs specifics in osd-ldiskfs and zfs specifics in osd-zfs.
Then how to keep the combined EA inside osd-ldiskfs efficiently when migrated to ZFS?

Patch Set 12:

read by osd-ldiskfs and write by osd-zfs

Patch Set 12:

If have to read via osd-ldiskfs, and write via osd-zfs, then the client side copy may be the most simple solution...
Patch Set 12: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 12: Code-Review+2

parents.subject: LU-10187 osd-zfs: repair FID-in-dirent for ZFS backend

commit.subject: LU-10189 osd: handle PFID EA in LMA properly
commit.message:
LU-10189 osd: handle PFID EA in LMA properly

Originally, the issue was caused by old ldiskfs OST device
with 256-bytes sized inode. Because the inode inline space
was very limited, we have to store the PFID EA inside LMA
EA for stripe and PFL component information.

When we restore the OST from such old OST via server side
file level backup, then such composite LMA will be on the
new OST even if the new OST inode has enough inline space
to hold separated PFID EA.

In futher, if we migrate the old OST from ldiskfs to ZFS,
then such composite LMA will also be on the ZFS based OST
although the PFID EA can be stroed independently on ZFS.

So the OSD logic, in spite of ldiskfs or ZFS, needs to
understand the composite LMA EA, and handle it properly.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2b66787e725e13da7984f1bc2df45760dfbe4c4d
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/29696
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
